Ad Text:
DXC Technology (NYSE: DXC) is a leading enterprise technology and innovation partner delivering software, services, and solutions to global enterprises and public sector organizations — helping them harness AI to drive outcomes at a time of exponential change with speed. 

We are currently looking for an experienced SAP Integration Consultant – CPI to join our high performing Cloud and Platform team.  This is a permanent position ideally based in Sydney. 

In this role you will be involved in a variety of integration activities mainly focused across CPI and PI/PO supporting a range of clients.  Your outstanding communication skills will allow you to develop strong customer relationships and operate at a high-level within our Managed Services team.

To be successful you will require the following;

3 years exp, mainly SAP CPI, but some PI/PO experience is highly regarded.
Strong understanding of integration patterns, concepts and standards across enterprise applications
Experience in ABAP would be highly beneficial
Strong stakeholder engagement skills
Excellent interpersonal skills
Previous experience working in a consulting environment, working across either projects or BAU 
In alignment with our commitment to fostering a flexible and dynamic work environment, we have adopted a hybrid working approach that acknowledges the importance of in-person collaboration while recognising that the determination of your work location will be based on a range of factors to ensure both individual preferences and operational requirements are met.

If you would like to be part of a culture that drives innovation, delivers results, rewards performance and encourages ideas, then please press the "Apply Now" button to submit your resume.

About Icon Water

Icon Water is an iconic Canberra business with a history of providing essential services to Canberra for over 100 years.

We are the ACT’s supplier of essential water and wastewater services and have proudly served our community for over 100 years – sourcing, treating and supplying water, and managing Canberra’s wastewater network. Our work is essential to the health, prosperity and liveability of the region, and we are committed to environmental, social and financial sustainability in everything we do.

Icon Water is a purpose driven organisation where everyone contributes to our core business, ensuring high quality drinking water and sustainable wastewater treatment. Icon Water’s culture is driven by our values of care, connection, community and courage. These are values we see in action every day as we challenge ourselves to meet the expectations of our community.  

Icon Water is an Equal Opportunity Employer which values diversity and inclusion. We encourage applicants from all backgrounds, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islanders, people with disabilities, people from culturally diverse backgrounds and all ages. We also have a strong focus on gender balance within the organisation and encourage women to apply. 

 




About this role:  We are currently looking to fill two Senior Business Analyst positions. 

These roles are responsible for leading the creation of key outputs for one or more projects delivered by the Digital Delivery Branch, Digital Technology Group. More specifically, these roles have responsibility for supporting staff transition to new ways of working, focussing on systems and processes relating to Icon Waters’ Digital environment which includes Operational Technology (OT), Cloud and on-premise based platforms.
 

Other Key Responsibilities Include:

Engaging with business areas to elicit and document business challenges and objectives independently, with minimal supervision 
Establish a strong working relationship with each client based on clear expectation-setting and building of mutual trust 
Ensure that customer business objectives and scoping statements are well defined and understood by you, the project team and business areas 
Support the production of risk mitigation discussions and plans for any significant project risks 
Support stakeholders and Change Management teams in adopting changes and performance with new systems 
Identifies, investigates, documents and analyses business processes, procedures and work practices 
Identifies and evaluates inefficiencies and recommends optimal business practices, and system functionality and behaviour 
Creates end user and training documentation, and conducts formal training classes 
Develops functional and non-functional specifications for use by system developers and other third parties
Uses data and process modelling techniques to create clear system specifications for the design and development of system software 
Acts as a central reference point for project information, providing guidance and assistance in the project decision making process 
Act as a mentor to other Business Analyst team members 
Work within and support the Icon Water environment and culture. 
Assist in the improvement of Icon Water project processes, documentation, and templates. 
Other duties within the employee’s skill, competence, and training. 

Who we are looking for:  We are looking for suitable candidates who hold relevant tertiary qualifications in IT, Business or related disciplines

You should also have the following experience: 

10+ years' experience as a Senior (Digital) Business Analyst
Exceptional written and verbal communication skills; active listening skills and attention to detail 
Ability to produce high quality stakeholder communications 
Able to manage multiple deliverables simultaneously and with minimal supervision 
Experience in business re-engineering and process design 
Experience in engaging, influencing, negotiating, presenting and collaborating with a range of business stakeholders to develop ongoing working relationships that achieve positive outcomes 
Experience in complex, dynamic, fast paced environments where new problems and issues frequently arise which require new methods to be created 
Knowledge of risk and quality management practices in a digital project context 
Knowledge of the business of a regulated utility, its structure and operations

To find out more about the role you can view the detailed Position Description or on the Icon Water's Career Portal. 

You can also contact Nic••••••••@iconwater.com.au via email for further information. 

Please submit your resume and cover letter detailing your claims for this position through our Careers Portal by Sunday 24th May 2026.

As one of Australia's Top Tier Investment Groups, this organisation is committed to building a high performance focused culture and working environment, as well as actively developing and encouraging its team for longer term individual career opportunities.  Growth and an increased demand for quality investment performance analytics information from the investment team have driven the requirement for this newly created Senior Investment Performance Analyst position.

As part of the investments team, the  Senior Investment Performance Analyst will be focused on a variety of challenging investment analytics work to support the investment team, as well as ensuring that the investment performance, attribution and risk analytics reporting needs of the organisation are met through the establishment of flexible investment reporting and information delivery frameworks. Your challenges will you will also be primarily responsible for more complex attribution analysis and risk analytics adding value to the front office investment management team.

To be successful you will have previous Investment Performance experience (3 - 5 years +) from either from the investment management, superannuation, investment consulting or custody with hands-on technical performance skills and proactive communication skills allowing you to interact successfully at senior levels of the investment team.  Some previous experience in investment performance projects and the ability to understand, analyse and resolve more complex financial market instruments will be well regarded.  A good academic profile is ideal with postgraduate studies in either CFA or Masters being highly regarded.

The Opportunity

A highly regarded Australian organisation is embarking on an industry-defining, multi-year transformation and automation program.

There is a strong focus on modernising enterprise systems, streamlining finance operations, and operating at the forefront of AI-enabled capability.

This is a rare opportunity for a Senior Data Engineer to play a critical role in shaping the future-state data and automation landscape of a large, complex business.

Working across enterprise platforms, finance systems, and modern cloud technologies, you will help design and implement scalable data solutions that unlock automation, improve decision-making, and enable intelligent operational outcomes.

You’ll join a collaborative transformation environment where technology, data, and AI are becoming central to how the organisation operates and grows.

The Role

Reporting into a broader transformation and data function, this role will partner closely with Finance, Technology, and enterprise stakeholders to deliver modern data engineering and automation solutions across critical business processes.

You will be responsible for designing and building robust data pipelines, integrating enterprise systems, improving data quality and controls, and delivering scalable automation capabilities across a modern Microsoft data ecosystem.

This role is ideally suited to someone who enjoys operating in complex environments, can balance strategic thinking with hands-on delivery, and is excited by the opportunity to influence large-scale transformation outcomes.

Key Responsibilities
Design, build, and optimise scalable data pipelines and integrations across enterprise platforms
Develop automation frameworks and reusable engineering patterns to support long-term transformation objectives
Partner closely with business stakeholders to deliver process automation initiatives across core operational and finance workflows
Build and maintain curated datasets, semantic models, and reporting foundations that support enterprise analytics and AI capability
Implement monitoring, reconciliation, observability, and data quality controls to ensure reliability and accuracy
Contribute to future-state architecture and integration standards across the broader technology landscape
Collaborate with internal teams, vendors, and platform owners to manage system dependencies and releases
Support uplift in engineering capability through documentation, knowledge sharing, and best-practice delivery standards
About You

You are a technically strong Senior Data Engineer with proven experience delivering enterprise-grade data and integration solutions in complex environments.

You will bring

Strong experience designing and delivering modern data pipelines, ETL processes, and enterprise integrations
Advanced SQL capability and strong understanding of data modelling and warehousing concepts
Experience working within Microsoft Azure and/or Microsoft Fabric environments
Exposure to enterprise ERP and finance-related data environments
Strong understanding of automation, reconciliation, monitoring, and operational support practices
Experience working across APIs, messaging, and multiple enterprise applications
Strong stakeholder engagement skills with the ability to translate business requirements into technical solutions
A pragmatic, outcomes-focused mindset with a genuine sense of ownership
Why Apply?
Be part of a large-scale, business-critical transformation program
Work at the forefront of automation and emerging AI capability
Join a business investing heavily in modern technology and data platforms
High levels of visibility and engagement with senior leadership
Opportunity to influence enterprise-wide transformation outcomes
Collaborative and high-performing environment with strong long-term potential
If you’re looking for an opportunity to combine deep technical engineering capability with enterprise transformation impact, we’d love to hear from you.
Apply now or reach out for a confidential discussion.

About Waste Services Group (WSG)

Waste Services Group (‘WSG’) is one of Australia’s largest integrated providers of managed services, collections, specialised services and post-collections services to the SME resource recovery market.

We connect our customers with tailored solutions they can rely on. Taking the challenge of making every day more sustainable.

About the role

Join us on our ESG journey. Waste Services Group (WSG) is looking for a talented person to join its ESG team to support its sustainability agenda. We are looking for a data-driven professional who is passionate about sustainability and resource recovery, to deliver sustainability data and analysis, support sustainability reporting, as well as support delivery of sustainability projects and communications.

The ESG Data Manager will develop and implement strategies and initiatives to support the data management and reporting of various sustainability performance information across WSG. The role will have responsibility for activities to establish data governance, and ensure that data is accurate, timely and complete, to underpin GHG emissions and other ESG reporting. The role will support project coordination and have an opportunity to lead or contribute into sustainability initiatives. The successful applicant will be a subject matter expert in relation to GHG emissions and other sustainability data, providing insight and direction on issues or opportunities, and supporting delivery of sustainability reporting and initiatives.




Experience and Skills

Tertiary qualification (Engineering, Environmental Science, Accounting)

4-6 years of work experience, preferably in a similar role

Experience in the waste / resource recovery sector will be well regarded

Experience preparing sustainability reporting

Experience managing sustainability data and data structures

Strong attention-to-detail

Strong reconciliation skills

Strong written and verbal communication and presentation skills




Why WSG?

All the WSG businesses stem from individuals with strong entrepreneurship and customer focus, and a belief that the people in the business are the most important part of their business. Even though these businesses have joined to become WSG, our culture still holds the intimacy of a family-business, the nimbleness of being entrepreneurial and outcomes-focused offering the flexibility to achieve your goals in the way you like. With an incredible growth trajectory, our cultural focus is to maintain what we have, while we professionalize for scalability.

We live our values, all day, every day.

Wellbeing – flu shots and EAP

Career development - development plans that incorporate in-house training, mentoring and on-the-job skill development and coaching

Competitive salary package

If you don’t tick every box in this job description, please don’t rule yourself out. Research suggests that women and other people in underrepresented groups tend to only apply if they meet every requirement. We focus on hiring people who value inclusion, collaboration, adaptability, courage, and integrity, rather than ticking boxes – so if this resonates with you, then please apply.

Company: KBR - Kellogg Brown & Root Pty Ltd
Ad Text:
KBR is seeking Change Management Specialists at both mid and senior levels to support governance, control, and change tracking across major capital programs.

This role is critical in ensuring project changes are managed accurately, consistently, and in line with established governance frameworks.

Key Responsibilities:

Manage and support project change control processes

Maintain accurate change records covering scope, cost, and schedule

Coordinate approvals and stage‑gate processes

Support monthly reporting and change registers

Collaborate across Cost, Schedule, and Project Management disciplines

Maintain data integrity across project systems

Identify opportunities to improve change workflows and reporting

What You’ll Bring:

Degree‑qualified in Engineering, Project Management, or related discipline

Change Management Specialist: 3–5+ years’ experience

Senior Specialist: 8+ years’ experience

Strong understanding of governance and approval workflows

Experience with enterprise project systems (e.g. CAPS, Ellipse, Maximo)

High attention to detail and strong reporting capability

Why Join Us?

At KBR, change management is recognised as a critical project discipline, not an administrative function. You’ll be trusted to take ownership, influence governance outcomes, and contribute directly to delivery success.

You’ll gain exposure to complex delivery environments, structured career development, and the support of a global organisation with strong local leadership and a collaborative culture.

KBR Values:

Our KBR Values - We Value Our People, We Deliver, We Are People of Integrity, We Empower, We Are a Team of Teams - embody our people centric organisation.

Our culture, and our genuine focus on our people is a major factor in our success and we want you to be part of our journey. 

Help shape tomorrow by applying today!

About Us

At Rezdy, we’re builders, doers, and difference-makers, driven by a shared mission to reshape the tours, activities, and experiences industry. Alongside our sister brands, Checkfront and Regiondo, we power more than 20,000 businesses and support over $10B in bookings globally. Our technology helps operators thrive while delivering unforgettable moments to travelers around the world.

We work in an industry built on adventure, energy, and human connection, and that same spirit fuels how we show up every day. Spanning North America, Europe, and Asia-Pacific, our teams are united by bold goals, a bias for action, and an unwavering commitment to delivering for our customers.

Our success starts with people. Our teams are the engine behind everything we create. We value self-starters who take ownership, embrace challenges, and raise the bar, for themselves and those around them. We believe in creating space to grow, take risks, and make a real impact, and we celebrate those who lead with curiosity, grit, and drive.

If you’re passionate about performance, hungry to learn, and ready to help shape the future of travel—this is your kind of place. Let’s build, grow, and win together.


About the Role

We are looking for a Senior Data Engineer with 5+ years of experience to join our data team. Our data platform powers analytics for both internal business users and external customers, and plays a key role in enabling data-driven decision making across the organization.

In this role, you will primarily focus on maintaining and enhancing our existing data platform, ensuring reliability, performance, and scalability. You will also work closely with internal stakeholders to design and implement new data models in our Enterprise Data Warehouse as new reporting and analytics requirements emerge.

The platform currently supports the data and analytics needs across Rezdy, Checkfront, and Regiondo, and provides reporting capabilities to both operational teams and customers.

Our modern data stack includes Google BigQuery, Fivetran, dbt, Metabase & GoodData. This is a great opportunity for someone who enjoys working with mature data platforms, improving existing pipelines, and delivering high-quality data models that directly support business outcomes.

This is a one year fixed contract hybrid position based in Sydney, Australia. Candidates must have Australian permanent residency or citizenship to be considered.


What you will do

Maintain and support the enterprise data platform and existing data pipelines.

Ensure reliable data ingestion, transformation, and reporting across Rezdy, Checkfront, and Regiondo.

Monitor, troubleshoot, and resolve issues across the data stack to ensure data accuracy and availability.

Work closely with internal business users to understand their reporting and analytics requirements.

Design and implement new data models in BigQuery using dbt based on business needs.

Maintain and manage data ingestion pipelines.

Deliver clean, well-structured datasets that power reporting and dashboards in GoodData & Google sheets.

Optimize BigQuery queries and data models for performance and cost efficiency.

Ensure data quality, documentation, and governance across the data platform.

Contribute to improving data engineering standards, monitoring, and platform reliability.


What we are looking for

5+ years of experience in data engineering or analytics engineering roles.

Strong experience with SQL and data modeling in modern cloud data warehouses.

Hands-on experience with Google BigQuery.

Experience using dbt for transformation and data modeling.

Experience with data ingestion tools such as Fivetran or similar ELT platforms.

Experience supporting analytics platforms or BI tools.

Strong understanding of data warehousing concepts and dimensional modeling.

Experience maintaining and operating production data pipelines and platforms.

Ability to work with business stakeholders to translate requirements into scalable data models.

Strong troubleshooting and problem-solving skills.

Good documentation and communication skills.

When you join our team, you’re stepping into a culture built on momentum, ownership, and clarity of purpose. We move decisively, think boldly, and stay deeply connected to our customers and the people behind the work. Across all our brands, we believe meaningful impact comes from empowered teams, aligned priorities, and unwavering commitment.

Here’s what you can expect:

High trust, real impact: You’ll have the autonomy and expectation to lead with ownership, tackle problems end-to-end, and make decisions that move the business forward in meaningful ways.

Curiosity with discipline: We value asking sharp questions, challenging assumptions, and exploring smarter ways to work. We don’t just get things done, but to get them done right for our customers.

One team, all in: Collaboration beats ego, wins are shared, and we rally together when the work gets tough.

Space to grow: You’ll be supported and stretched, taking on challenges that build capability, sharpen judgment, and accelerate your growth as a leader and problem-solver.

Progress with purpose: We move fast, stay focused on what truly matters, and prioritise long-term impact over quick fixes.

We are Coca-ColaEuropacificPartners (CCEP),42,000 people across 31 countries, working together to make, move, and sell some of the world’s most loved beverages. As a leading global consumer goods company, we support 4 million customers and continue to invest in new products and innovation to delight the 600 million people who enjoy our drinks each day.

In Australia, our portfolio includes the Coca-Cola family of products and well-loved brands such as Sprite, Fanta, Monster and Mother Energy, Mount Franklin, Powerade, Billson’s, and Grinders Coffee.

The opportunity   

This is a high-impact, Chief of Staff-style role within CCEP’s in-house Strategy & Consulting team, part of the Commercial Excellence Office.

It is offered on a permanent full-time basis and located at our head office in North Sydney. 

You will operate at the heart of the Commercial leadership team, partnering closely with senior executives to shape strategy, drive planning rhythm, and elevate decision-making. 

This role is about partnering with leadership to shape the narrative, manage board reporting and APS scorecards, and enable focus on the priorities that drive growth.

Your responsibilities 

Act as a trusted advisor and thought partner to senior Commercial leaders

Own and elevate executive-level storytelling; CEO and MD presentations, board materials, sales conferences, second circle, and roadshows

Translate data, insights, and strategy into clear, compelling narratives for leadership forums

Lead the rhythm of the business (reporting, scorecards, APS, performance tracking), ensuring a strong narrative sits behind the numbers

Support Australian and European LT engagements and key moments, crafting aligned messaging and strategic positioning

Identify priorities, manage trade-offs, and ensure focus on highest-impact initiatives

Operate as a “force multiplier” for leadership, reducing time spent on operational tasks and enabling greater focus on strategic growth

Step into “project sprints” when needed to drive pace, alignment and outcomes, focusing on solving complex challenges across categories, inorganic growth, and AI opportunities

About you

5 to 10 years’ experience in strategy, consulting, or corporate roles with exposure to Partners or C-suite stakeholders

Prior reporting experience, including board-level, APS scorecards and executive materials

Strong storytelling and communication skills; able to simplify complexity into clear, actionable messages

Highly commercial and strategic thinker with strong business acumen

Proven ability to influence senior stakeholders and operate with credibility at executive level

Comfortable operating in ambiguity, pace, and evolving priorities

Strong planning, structuring, and problem-solving capability

Advanced skills in PowerPoint, Excel, and data interpretation

Experience in Big 4 or MBB environments highly regarded, but not essential

What’s in it for you

Work at the centre of enterprise-level decision-making

Gain exposure to ELT, board, and major strategic initiatives

Play a critical role in shaping the future direction of the commercial business

Be part of a high-performing, internal strategy capability

Clear progression opportunities within a rapidly expanding organisation

We also offer generous monthly product allowance and access a wide range of corporate discount programs, including Fitness Passport, Just Sunnies, QANTAS Club, Microsoft, Lenovo, Hertz, GoGet and Estee Lauder  

Opportunity to join the CCEP Employee Share Purchase Plan

Coca-Cola Europacific Partners is committed to an inclusive, equitable and safe workplace where everyone is supported. If you need any accessibility adjustments during the process, please let us know. Your wellbeing matters to us.

When you join CCEP, you’re stepping into a global organisation known for developing exceptional talent. We invest deeply in learning, mobility and long term growth, creating an environment where your ambition is backed and your career can thrive.

This role is subject to standard preemployment checks, which may include references, working rights, background checks and/or medical assessments.

Job Description


Join the team redefining how the world experiences design.
Hey, g'day, mabuhay, kia ora, 你好, hallo, vítejte!

Thanks for stopping by. We know job hunting can be a little time-consuming, and you're probably keen to find out what's on offer, so we'll get straight to the point.

Where and how you can work
Our flagship campus is in Sydney. We also have a campus in Melbourne and co-working spaces in Brisbane, Perth and Adelaide. But you have a choice in where and how you work, we trust our Canvanauts to choose the balance that empowers them and their team to achieve their goals.

What you'd be doing in this role
As Canva scales, change continues to be part of our DNA. But we like to think that’s all part of the fun. This will give you a flavour of the types of things you’ll be working on when you start — though it will likely evolve over time.

At the moment, this role is focused on

Define the CJKI and International Data Vision & Strategy: Own and shape the end-to-end data strategy for China, Japan, South Korea, and India. Establish how we measure success across highly diverse and complex markets - defining scalable frameworks, region-specific KPIs, and long-term growth metrics that balance global consistency with local nuance.
Act as a Strategic Thought Partner to Regional Leadership: Serve as the senior analytics partner to CJKI country leaders and regional marketing leads. Influence investment decisions, market expansion priorities, and localisation strategy through forward-looking, insight-driven recommendations - not just reporting performance, but shaping direction.
Translate Regional Complexity into Global Influence: Ensure the voice of CJKI users is embedded in Canva’s global product and growth strategy. Synthesise behavioural trends, monetisation dynamics, ecosystem constraints, and experimentation learnings into clear inputs that influence roadmap decisions.
Identify Structural Growth Levers: Go beyond surface metrics to uncover structural drivers of growth and friction - including lifecycle drop-offs, pricing sensitivity, ecosystem dynamics, regulatory constraints, and localisation gaps unique to CJKI markets.
Set the Regional Measurement & Experimentation Bar: Define best-in-class experimentation and measurement standards across the not only the region but at Canva. Guide the design, interpretation, and rollout of experiments, pilots, and feature launches across multiple markets - ensuring speed, rigour, and long-term learning.
Build Scalable Regional Data Foundations: Oversee the evolution of trusted, scalable data products that provide visibility into user growth, retention, monetisation, campaign performance, and product adoption across CJKI and International ensuring the region operates on strong, decision-ready data infrastructure.
Elevate Craft Across International Data: Act as a force multiplier within the International Data team, mentoring Senior Data Scientists, setting quality standards, sharing reusable frameworks, and influencing best practices across regions.

You're probably a match if

You have 8+ years in data, growth, or product analytics, with impact that spans your team and influences adjacent teams; with demonstrated ownership of a major region, business line, or strategic domain.
You’ve operated as a trusted advisor to c-level stakeholders, influencing decisions at leadership level rather than solely delivering analysis.
You think in systems - connecting product, marketing, monetisation, lifecycle, and market dynamics into coherent growth strategies.
You're fluent in English and speak Mandarin Chinese, Japanese, or Korean - this helps you collaborate deeply with the local teams and understand the cultural nuances that shape how our product lands in market.
You define long-term measurement philosophies and success frameworks, not just dashboards.
You design and critique complex experimentation programs (multi-market rollouts, counter-metrics, guardrails, power trade-offs) and translate results into clear strategic action.
You proactively identify ambiguous, undefined problems and create clarity, alignment, and forward motion.
You balance global scalability with local nuance - knowing when to standardise and when to localise.
You communicate complex ideas with clarity and conviction to diverse audiences, from country managers to global product leaders.
You uplift the craft: share reusable code and documentation, review PRs/queries, and mentor other data scientists informally to scale good practices.
You actively leverage AI in your day-to-day work to accelerate insights, improve quality, and increase impact.
You bring deep expertise in SQL and Python and are highly proficient in modern analytics stacks (e.g., Snowflake, Mode, NetSpring, Omni) and experimentation platforms (e.g., Pilot or similar).

Nice‑to‑Haves

Experience working in one or more CJKI markets.
Understanding of platform ecosystems, localisation strategy, and regulatory considerations in China, Japan, South Korea, or India.
Experience supporting international product expansion or market entry.
Passion for Canva’s mission and curiosity about how technology adoption varies across cultures.

About the team
This role sits within the International Data Team - a distributed group of Data Scientists who exist to deliver a high-quality understanding of our markets, products, and users. We turn data into clarity so Canva makes the right decisions at the right time.

CJKI represents one of Canva’s most strategically important and complex growth opportunities. The region spans diverse languages, regulatory environments, digital ecosystems, and user behaviours - requiring thoughtful regional strategy deeply connected to our global platform.

As the Staff Data Scientist for International, you’ll be the senior analytics voice - shaping how we define success, unlocking structural growth opportunities, and ensuring regional insights meaningfully influence Canva’s global direction.

If you’re excited about operating at the intersection of regional nuance and global scale - and building systems that influence company-wide strategy - this is your opportunity.

What's in it for you?
Achieving our crazy big goals motivates us to work hard - and we do - but you’ll experience lots of moments of magic, connectivity and fun woven throughout life at Canva, too. We also offer a range of benefits to set you up for every success in and outside of work.

Here’s a taste of what’s on offer:
• Equity packages - we want our success to be yours too
• Inclusive parental leave policy that supports all parents & carers
• An annual Vibe & Thrive allowance to support your wellbeing, social connection, office setup & more
• Flexible leave options that empower you to be a force for good, take time to recharge and supports you personally

Check out lifeatcanva.com for more info.

Other stuff to know
We make hiring decisions based on your experience, skills and passion, as well as how you can enhance Canva and our culture. When you apply, please tell us the pronouns you use and any reasonable adjustments you may need during the interview process.

We celebrate all types of skills and backgrounds at Canva so even if you don’t feel like your skills quite match what’s listed above - we still want to hear from you!

Please note that interviews are conducted virtually.
